architecture.summary: CB-LLM transforms pretrained LLMs into interpretable models by inserting a Concept Bottleneck Layer (CBL) between the backbone's hidden representations and task-specific heads. For classification, text embeddings pass through CBL (trained to align with concept scores), ReLU, then a sparse linear layer for class logits. For generation, CBL and an unsupervised layer are concatenated and fed to an unembedding head; adversarial training forces the unsupervised layer to remove concept information, preserving CBL's steerability. Concept scores are derived from sentence embeddings (all-mpnet-base-v2) and refined by masking misaligned class–concept pairs.
trained_components: final linear classification layer (with sparsity constraint)
linear concept detector (adversarial, generation only, discarded at inference)

claims.statement: CB-LLM w/ ACC achieves test accuracy competitive with or matching finetuned black-box RoBERTa-base across SST2, YelpP, AGnews, and DBpedia
Automatic Concept Scoring (ACS) takes 1.6 hours on YelpP and DBpedia datasets (largest), versus 8.8 hours for LLM-based labeling on just 1,000 samples per dataset
CB-LLM w/ ACC achieves higher average human rating on Activation Faithfulness task compared to TBMC3M baseline
CB-LLM (generation) achieves concept detection accuracy within ~3% of Llama3-8B finetuned for direct concept classification
CB-LLM (generation) achieves significantly higher steerability score with adversarial training (Module 2) versus without, where steerability without adversarial training is close to random
CB-LLM (generation) achieves perplexity comparable to standard finetuned Llama3-8B, indicating no degradation in generation quality
Toxicity detection chatbot achieves 92% accuracy on toxicity detection and 91% steerability score on test set

CB-LLM enables concept unlearning, allowing users to deactivate neurons in CBL to remove biased or subjective concepts; case study shows 2,726 out of 10k test samples shifted from negative to positive after unlearning 'overpriced' concept
